channel.title: CSOH - Cloud Security News
channel.description: Latest cloud security news curated by Cloud Security Office Hours. Covers AWS, Azure, GCP, Kubernetes vulnerabilities, breaches, and more.

item.title: New Microsoft Defender “RedSun” zero-day PoC grants SYSTEM privileges
item.description: A researcher known as "Chaotic Eclipse" has published a proof-of-concept exploit for a second Microsoft Defender zero-day, dubbed "RedSun," in the past two weeks, protesting how the company works with cybersecurity re...

item.title: North Korea Uses ClickFix to Target macOS Users' Data
item.description: Sapphire Sleet uses fake job offers and phony Zoom updates to deliver ClickFix attacks that steal credentials and sensitive data from Macs.

item.title: Cisco fixed four critical flaws in Identity Services and Webex
item.description: Cisco fixed four critical flaws in Identity Services and Webex that could allow code execution and user impersonation. Cisco has addressed four critical vulnerabilities affecting its Identity Services and Webex platfo...

item.title: US Nationals Jailed for Operating Fake Remote Worker Laptop Farms for North Korea
item.description: US authorities jail two Americans for aiding North Korean laptop farm scams that infiltrated over 100 firms

item.title: APK Malformation Found in Thousands of Android Malware Samples
item.description: APK malformation tactic now appears in over 3000 Android malware samples evading static analysis

item.title: Two-Factor Authentication Breaks Free from the Desktop
item.description: Threat actors know how to bypass security systems outside of traditional IT environments. Implementing 2FA could provide a needed extra security barrier in the physical world.

item.title: Google expands Gemini AI use to fight malicious ads on its platform
item.description: Google says it is increasingly using its Gemini AI models to detect and block harmful ads on its advertising platforms, as scammers and threat actors continue to evolve their tactics to evade detection. [...]

item.title: Microsoft's Original Windows Secure Boot Certificate Is Expiring
item.description: The Secure Boot refresh is one of the largest coordinated security maintenance efforts across the Windows ecosystem, Microsoft said. Update those PCs soon.

item.title: Anthropic releases Claude Opus 4.7 with automated cybersecurity safeguards
item.description: Software teams building agentic AI workflows have been pushing frontier models toward longer, unsupervised task runs. Claude Opus 4.7, now generally available from Anthropic, is aimed squarely at that demand, with par...

item.title: Cookeville Medical Center Notifies Patients After July 2025 Ransomware Attack
item.description: Tennessee's CRMC notifies over 337,000 patients of Rhysida ransomware breach exposing sensitive data

item.title: OpenAI Widens Access to Cybersecurity Model After Anthropic’s Mythos Reveal
item.description: GPT‑5.4‑Cyber is a model fine-tuned for defenders, lowering boundaries for legitimate cybersecurity work. The post OpenAI Widens Access to Cybersecurity Model After Anthropic’s Mythos Reveal appeared first on Security...

item.title: New ATHR vishing platform uses AI voice agents for automated attacks
item.description: A new cybercrime platform called ATHR can harvest credentials via fully automated voice phishing attacks that use both human operators and AI agents for the social engineering phase. [...]

item.title: Most "AI SOCs" Are Just Faster Triage. That's Not Enough.
item.description: AI-powered SOC tools promise automation, but most only speed up triage instead of reducing real workload. Tines shows how real gains come from end-to-end workflows that execute actions across systems, not just summari...

item.title: AI platform n8n abused for stealthy phishing and malware delivery
item.description: Attackers abuse AI automation platform n8n to run phishing campaigns, deliver malware, and evade security by using trusted infrastructure. Threat actors are exploiting the popular AI workflow automation platform n8n t...

item.title: ThreatsDay Bulletin: Defender 0-Day, SonicWall Brute-Force, 17-Year-Old Excel RCE and 15 More Stories
item.description: You know that feeling when you open your feed on a Thursday morning and it's just... a lot? Yeah. This week delivered. We've got hackers getting creative in ways that are almost impressive if you ignore the whole "cri...

item.title: CISA Adds One Known Exploited Vulnerability to Catalog
item.description: CISA has added one new vulnerability to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) Catalog , based on evidence of active exploitation. CVE-2026-34197 Apache ActiveMQ Improper Input Validation Vulnerability This type of...

item.title: Cisco Patches Critical Vulnerabilities in Webex, ISE
item.description: The flaws can be exploited remotely to impersonate users or execute arbitrary commands on the underlying OS. The post Cisco Patches Critical Vulnerabilities in Webex, ISE appeared first on SecurityWeek .

item.title: PowMix botnet targets Czech workforce
item.description: Cisco Talos discovered an ongoing malicious campaign, operating since at least December 2025, affecting a broader workforce in the Czech Republic with a previously undocumented botnet we call “PowMix.”

item.title: More than pretty pictures: Wendy Bishop on visual storytelling in tech
item.description: Wendy shares the unique challenges and rewards of bridging the gap between artistic expression and highly technical research.

item.title: Human Trust of AI Agents
item.description: Interesting research: “ Humans expect rationality and cooperation from LLM opponents in strategic games .” Abstract: As Large Language Models (LLMs) integrate into our social and economic interactions, we need to deep...

item.title: From clinics to government: UAC-0247 expands cyber campaign across Ukraine
item.description: CERT-UA reports UAC-0247 targeting Ukrainian clinics and government bodies with malware stealing data from Chromium browsers and WhatsApp. CERT-UA has revealed a cyber campaign by the threat actor UAC-0247 targeting U...

item.title: Ransomware Hits Automotive Data Expert Autovista
item.description: The automotive analysis and data company is working with external experts to investigate the attack. The post Ransomware Hits Automotive Data Expert Autovista appeared first on SecurityWeek .

item.title: Automotive Ransomware Attacks Double in a Year
item.description: Halcyon says ransomware now accounts for more than two-fifths of cyber-attacks targeting carmakers

item.title: Claude Code, Gemini CLI, GitHub Copilot Agents Vulnerable to Prompt Injection via Comments
item.description: A researcher has disclosed the details of the AI attack method he has named ‘Comment and Control’. The post Claude Code, Gemini CLI, GitHub Copilot Agents Vulnerable to Prompt Injection via Comments appeared first on...

item.title: Microsoft: April Windows Server 2025 update may fail to install
item.description: Microsoft is investigating an issue causing this month's KB5082063 security update to fail to install on some Windows Server 2025 systems. [...]

item.title: Sweden reports cyberattack attempt on heating plant amid rising energy threats
item.description: Sweden says a pro-Russian group attacked a heating plant in 2025. The failed cyberattack highlights growing threats to Europe’s energy infrastructure. Sweden has blamed a pro-Russian group linked to Russian intelligen...

item.title: Frontier AI for Defenders: CrowdStrike and OpenAI TAC
Cloud security has hit its human limits: Key takeaways from the 2026 Cloud-Native Security and Usage Report
item.description: Cloud security has reached a turning point. As environments scale and attackers move at machine speed, human-driven workflows can no longer keep up. The 2026 Cloud-Native Security and Usage Report reveals how leading...

item.title: WordPress plugin suite hacked to push malware to thousands of sites
item.description: More than 30 WordPress plugins in the EssentialPlugin package have been compromised with malicious code that allows unauthorized access to websites running them. [...]

item.title: Navigating the Unique Security Risks of Asia's Digital Supply Chain
item.description: Regulatory differences, interconnected digital ecosystems, and the rise of AI have created a complex supply chain Asian organizations must wrangle.

item.title: European Cybersecurity Agency ENISA Seeks Top-Tier Status in CVE Program
item.description: The EU cybersecurity agency looks to become the third Top-Level Root CVE Numbering Authority, alongside CISA and MITRE

item.title: CISA flags Windows Task Host vulnerability as exploited in attacks
item.description: CISA warned U.S. government agencies to secure their systems against a Windows Task Host privilege escalation vulnerability that could allow attackers to gain SYSTEM privileges. [...]

item.title: U.S. CISA adds Microsoft SharePoint Server, and Microsoft Office Excel flaws to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog
item.description: U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) adds Microsoft SharePoint Server, and Microsoft Office Excel flaws to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog. The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure...

item.title: Project Think: building the next generation of AI agents on Cloudflare
item.description: Announcing a preview of the next edition of the Agents SDK — from lightweight primitives to a batteries-included platform for AI agents that think, act, and persist.

item.title: Introducing Agent Lee - a new interface to the Cloudflare stack
item.description: Agent Lee is an in-dashboard agent that shifts Cloudflare’s interface from manual tab-switching to a single prompt. Using sandboxed TypeScript, it helps you troubleshoot and manage your stack as a grounded technical c...

item.title: Register domains wherever you build: Cloudflare Registrar API now in beta
item.description: The Cloudflare Registrar API is now in beta. Developers and AI agents can search, check availability, and register domains at cost directly from their editor, their terminal, or their agent — without leaving their wor...

item.title: Browser Run: give your agents a browser
item.description: Browser Rendering is now Browser Run, with Live View, Human in the Loop, CDP access, session recordings, and 4x higher concurrency limits for AI agents.

item.title: Rearchitecting the Workflows control plane for the agentic era
item.description: Cloudflare Workflows, a durable execution engine for multi-step applications, now supports higher concurrency and creation rate limits through a rearchitectured control plane, helping scale to meet the use cases for d...

item.title: Critical Nginx-ui MCP Flaw Actively Exploited in the Wild
item.description: Critical nginx-ui MCP authentication bypass CVE-2026-33032 actively exploited with CVSS 9.8

item.title: Actively Exploited nginx-ui Flaw (CVE-2026-33032) Enables Full Nginx Server Takeover
item.description: A recently disclosed critical security flaw impacting nginx-ui, an open-source, web-based Nginx management tool, has come under active exploitation in the wild. The vulnerability in question is CVE-2026-33032 (CVSS sc...

item.title: April Patch Tuesday Fixes Critical Flaws Across SAP, Adobe, Microsoft, Fortinet, and More
item.description: A number of critical vulnerabilities impacting products from Adobe, Fortinet, Microsoft, and SAP have taken center stage in April's Patch Tuesday releases. Topping the list is an SQL injection vulnerability impacting...

item.title: The n8n n8mare: How threat actors are misusing AI workflow automation
item.description: Cisco Talos research has uncovered agentic AI workflow automation platform abuse in emails. Recently, we identified an increase in the number of emails that abuse n8n, one of these platforms, from as early as October...

item.title: Researchers Spot Surge in Brute-Force Attacks from Middle East
item.description: Barracuda says 88% of brute-force attempts in Q1 were from the region

item.title: Microsoft Fixes Two Zero-Days in April Patch Tuesday
item.description: Microsoft has patched two zero-day flaws and over 160 others

item.title: Microsoft Issues Patches for SharePoint Zero-Day and 168 Other New Vulnerabilities
item.description: Microsoft on Tuesday released updates to address a record 169 security flaws across its product portfolio, including one vulnerability that has been actively exploited in the wild. Of these 169 vulnerabilities, 157 ar...

item.title: Sysdig Automations: Streamlining detection to response into a unified workflow
item.description: Sysdig Automations unifies fragmented cloud security workflows by connecting detection, investigation, and response into a single, cohesive system, reducing inefficiencies and manual effort. It enables teams to automa...

item.title: Kubernetes 1.36 - New security features
item.description: Kubernetes 1.36 will be released soon, bringing 60 enhancements. Of particular note is the effort made to improve dynamic resource allocation (DRA).In terms of security, there are 22 changes spanning admission control...

item.title: Masterclass: AI is more than ChatGPT and LLMs
item.description: Artificial intelligence is trendy, and it seems like it’s everything tech companies talk about.It also seems like it’s something completely new.

item.title: How Financial Services Organizations Can Stay Compliant Without Sacrificing Security
item.description: The stakes couldn’t be higher for financial services organizations. They have to protect customers’ money and privacy, while complying with…

item.title: Privilege Elevation Dominates Massive Microsoft Patch Update
item.description: Elevation-of-privilege bugs accounted for more than half of the 165 vulnerabilities patched, with two zero-days in that mix.

item.title: Microsoft Patch Tuesday for April 2026 - Snort Rule and Prominent Vulnerabilities
item.description: Overview of patch tuesday release from Microsoft for April 2026.

item.title: Upcoming Speaking Engagements
item.description: This is a current list of where and when I am scheduled to speak: I’m speaking at DemocracyXChange 2026 in Toronto, Ontario, Canada, on April 18, 2026. I’m speaking at the SANS AI Cybersecurity Summit 2026 in Arlingto...

item.title: Google Adds Rust-Based DNS Parser into Pixel 10 Modem to Enhance Security
item.description: Google has announced the integration of a Rust-based Domain Name System (DNS) parser into the modem firmware as part of its ongoing efforts to beef up the security of Pixel devices and push memory-safe code at a more...

item.title: AI-Driven Pushpaganda Scam Exploits Google Discover to Spread Scareware and Ad Fraud
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have unmasked a novel ad fraud scheme that has been found to leverage search engine poisoning (SEO) techniques and artificial intelligence (AI)-generated content to push deceptive news storie...

item.title: State-sponsored threats: Different objectives, similar access paths
item.description: A look at 2025 state-sponsored threats, exploring how actors linked to China, Russia, North Korea, and Iran use vulnerabilities, identity, and trusted access paths to achieve their goals.

item.title: CISOs Urged to Innovate with Talent Retention as Job Satisfaction Declines
item.description: A new IANS report claims just 34% of cybersecurity professionals plan to stay put in the next 12 months

item.title: Triad Nexus Expands Global Fraud Operations Despite US Sanctions
item.description: Triad Nexus scales $200m scams, uses infrastructure laundering, localized fraud and US-access blocks

item.title: Analysis of 216M Security Findings Shows a 4x Increase In Critical Risk (2026 Report)
item.description: OX Security recently analyzed 216 million security findings across 250 organizations over a 90-day period. The primary takeaway: while raw alert volume grew by 52% year-over-year, prioritized critical risk grew by nea...

item.title: 108 Malicious Chrome Extensions Steal Google and Telegram Data, Affecting 20,000 Users
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered a new campaign in which a cluster of 108 Google Chrome extensions has been found to communicate with the same command-and-control (C2) infrastructure with the goal of collecti...

item.title: Why Orgs Need to Test Networks to Withstand DDoS Attacks During Peak Loads
item.description: Security teams can't test distributed denial-of-service defenses in a vacuum. They need to test during periods of high demand, such as tax-filing deadlines.

item.title: Adobe Patches Actively Exploited Zero-Day That Lingered for Months
item.description: An attacker has been using maliciously crafted PDF files to exploit a zero-day in Adobe Acrobat and Reader for at least four months.

item.title: Empty Attestations: OT Lacks the Tools for Cryptographic Readiness
item.description: OT asset owners are being asked by regulators to attest to their post-quantum cryptographic readiness without the appropriate tooling, resulting in paperwork dressed up to look like genuine security.

item.title: JanelaRAT Malware Targets Latin American Banks with 14,739 Attacks in Brazil in 2025
item.description: Banks and financial institutions in Latin American countries like Brazil and Mexico have continued to be the target of a malware family called JanelaRAT. A modified version of BX RAT, JanelaRAT is known to steal finan...

item.title: APT41 Delivers 'Zero-Detection' Backdoor to Harvest Cloud Credentials
item.description: The prolific China-backed threat group is targeting AWS, Google, Azure, and Alibaba cloud environments and using typosquatting to obscure C2 communication.

item.title: Mailbox Rule Abuse Emerges as Stealthy Post-Compromise Threat
item.description: Attackers are abusing Microsoft 365 mailbox rules to hide activity, exfiltrate data and retain access after account compromise, researchers warn

item.title: FBI and Indonesian Police Dismantle W3LL Phishing Network Behind $20M Fraud Attempts
item.description: The U.S. Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI), in partnership with the Indonesian National Police, has dismantled the infrastructure associated with a global phishing operation that leveraged an off-the-shelf toolkit...

item.title: Mirax Android Trojan Turns Devices Into Residential Proxy Nodes
item.description: Security researchers warn of Mirax, an emerging Android banking trojan using MaaS, remote access and residential proxies to target European users

item.title: Building a CLI for all of Cloudflare
item.description: We’re introducing cf, a new unified CLI designed for consistency across the Cloudflare platform, alongside Local Explorer for debugging local data. These tools simplify how developers and AI agents interact with our n...

item.title: Durable Objects in Dynamic Workers: Give each AI-generated app its own database
item.description: We’re introducing Durable Object Facets, allowing Dynamic Workers to instantiate Durable Objects with their own isolated SQLite databases. This enables developers to build platforms that run persistent, stateful code...

item.title: Agents have their own computers with Sandboxes GA
item.description: Cloudflare Sandboxes give AI agents a persistent, isolated environment: a real computer with a shell, a filesystem, and background processes that starts on demand and picks up exactly where it left off.

item.title: ⚡ Weekly Recap: Fiber Optic Spying, Windows Rootkit, AI Vulnerability Hunting and More
item.description: Monday is back, and the weekend’s backlog of chaos is officially hitting the fan. We are tracking a critical zero-day that has been quietly living in your PDFs for months, plus some aggressive state-sponsored meddling...

item.title: Dynamic, identity-aware, and secure Sandbox auth
item.description: Outbound Workers for Sandboxes provide a programmable, zero-trust egress proxy for AI agents. This allows developers to inject credentials and enforce dynamic security policies without exposing sensitive tokens to unt...

item.title: AI Chatbots and Trust
item.description: All the leading AI chatbots are sycophantic, and that’s a problem : Participants rated sycophantic AI responses as more trustworthy than balanced ones. They also said they were more likely to come back to the flatteri...

item.title: North Korea's APT37 Uses Facebook Social Engineering to Deliver RokRAT Malware
item.description: The North Korean hacking group tracked as APT37 (aka ScarCruft) has been attributed to a fresh multi-stage, social engineering campaign in which threat actors approached targets on Facebook and added them as friends o...

item.title: UK Cyber Security Council Launches Associate Cyber Security Professional Title
item.description: The UK Cyber Security Council has unveiled a new Associate Cyber Security Professional title aimed at supporting early‑career cybersecurity professionals

item.title: Operation Atlantic Seizes $12m in Crypto Losses
item.description: UK, US and Canadian authorities have identified over 20,000 victims of approval phishing scams that trick users into handing over full crypto wallet access

item.title: OpenAI Revokes macOS App Certificate After Malicious Axios Supply Chain Incident
item.description: OpenAI revealed a GitHub Actions workflow used to sign its macOS apps led to the download of the malicious Axios library on March 31, but noted that no user data or internal system was compromised. "Out of an abundanc...

item.title: CPUID Breach Distributes STX RAT via Trojanized CPU-Z and HWMonitor Downloads
item.description: Unknown threat actors compromised CPUID ("cpuid[.]com"), a website that hosts popular hardware monitoring tools like CPU-Z, HWMonitor, HWMonitor Pro, and PerfMonitor, for less than 24 hours to serve malicious executab...

item.title: Adobe Patches Actively Exploited Acrobat Reader Flaw CVE-2026-34621
item.description: Adobe has released emergency updates to fix a critical security flaw in Acrobat Reader that has come under active exploitation in the wild. The vulnerability, assigned the CVE identifier CVE-2026-34621, carries a CVSS...

item.title: Hims Breach Exposes the Most Sensitive Kinds of PHI
item.description: Threat actors breached the telehealth brand, and now they may know patients' personal health details. What could they do with that information?

item.title: Your Next Breach Will Look Like Business as Usual
item.description: These are the fundamental detection model shifts cybersecurity teams need to make to keep up with the rising number of credential-based attacks.

item.title: [Video] The TTP Ep. 22: The Collapse of the Patch Window
item.description: In this episode of The Talos Threat Perspective, we discuss how vulnerability exploitation is accelerating, and why attacker speed, AI, and exposed systems are affecting the patch window.

item.title: Industrial Controllers Still Vulnerable As Conflicts Move to Cyber
item.description: The US government warns programmable logic controllers are being targeted, and research turns up 179 vulnerable operational technology (OT) devices.

item.title: GlassWorm Campaign Uses Zig Dropper to Infect Multiple Developer IDEs
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have flagged yet another evolution of the ongoing GlassWorm campaign, which employs a new Zig dropper that's designed to stealthily infect all integrated development environments (IDEs) on a...

item.title: Google Rolls Out DBSC in Chrome 146 to Block Session Theft on Windows
item.description: Google has made Device Bound Session Credentials (DBSC) generally available to all Windows users of its Chrome web browser, months after it began testing the security feature in open beta. The public availability is c...

item.title: Backdoored Smart Slider 3 Pro Update Distributed via Compromised Nextend Servers
item.description: Unknown threat actors have hijacked the update system for the Smart Slider 3 Pro plugin for WordPress and Joomla to push a poisoned version containing a backdoor. The incident impacts Smart Slider 3 Pro version 3.5.1....

item.title: Russia's 'Fancy Bear' APT Continues Its Global Onslaught
item.description: Victims don't need to match the cyber espionage group's technical sophistication, experts say. But patching and some form of zero trust are now non-negotiable.

item.title: 'BlueHammer' Windows Zero-Day Exploit Signals Microsoft Bug Disclosure Issues
item.description: Under the alias 'Chaotic Eclipse,' a researcher released a PoC exploit for a zero-day flaw that allows for system takeover by a local user, citing an undisclosed beef with Microsoft.

item.title: EngageLab SDK Flaw Exposed 50M Android Users, Including 30M Crypto Wallet Installs
item.description: Details have emerged about a now-patched security vulnerability in a widely used third-party Android software development kit (SDK) called EngageLab SDK that could have put millions of cryptocurrency wallet users at r...

item.title: Bitcoin Depot Reports $3.6m Crypto Theft After System Breach
item.description: Bitcoin Depot has disclosed a cyber-attack that led to the theft of more than 50 Bitcoin, worth $3.66m, after hackers accessed its internal systems

item.title: ThreatsDay Bulletin: Hybrid P2P Botnet, 13-Year-Old Apache RCE and 18 More Stories
item.description: Thursday. Another week, another batch of things that probably should've been caught sooner but weren't. This one's got some range — old vulnerabilities getting new life, a few "why was that even possible" moments, att...

item.title: From the field to the report and back again: How incident responders can use the Year in Review
item.description: The Year in Review distills Talos IR's observations into structured intelligence, but defenders should also be feeding this report back into their own preparation cycles. Here's how.

item.title: Google Warns of New Threat Group Targeting BPOs and Helpdesks
item.description: Google’s threat intel team warns UNC6783, a new extortion group possibly linked to the “Raccoon” persona, is targeting BPOs and enterprises

item.title: Marimo OSS Python Notebook RCE: From Disclosure to Exploitation in Under 10 Hours
item.description: On April 8, 2026, a critical vulnerability was disclosed in marimo, an open-source reactive Python notebook platform. Currently being tracked as GHSA-2679-6mx9-h9xc, it is a pre-authentication remote code execution (R...

item.title: A framework for securely collecting forensic artifacts into S3 buckets
item.description: When customers experience a security incident, they need to acquire forensic artifacts to identify root cause, extract indicators of compromise (IoCs), and validate remediation efforts. NIST 800-86, Guide to Integrati...

item.title: New Chaos Variant Targets Misconfigured Cloud Deployments, Adds SOCKS Proxy
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have flagged a new variant ofmalware called Chaosthat'scapable of hitting misconfigured cloud deployments, marking an expansion of the botnet's targeting infrastructure. "Chaos malware is inc...

item.title: Masjesu Botnet Emerges as DDoS-for-Hire Service Targeting Global IoT Devices
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have lifted the curtain on a stealthy botnet that's designed for distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks. Called Masjesu, the botnet has been advertised via Telegram as a DDoS-for-hire s...

item.title: Google API Keys Quietly Gain Access to Gemini on Android Devices
item.description: Google API key flaw exposes mobile apps to Gemini AI access, private files and billing risks

item.title: Critical Vulnerability in Ninja Forms Exposes WordPress Sites
item.description: Ninja Forms File Upload RCE via unauthenticated arbitrary file upload; update to 3.3.27 immediately

item.title: CVE-2026-23226: How a Missing Lock in ksmbd’s Channel List Exposes Your Linux SMB3 Server
item.description: Overview The Orca Security Research Pod discovered a use-after-free race condition in the Linux kernel’s ksmbd SMB3 server. When two connections share a session over SMB3 multichannel, the kernel can read a freed chan...

item.title: CISA Adds One Known Exploited Vulnerability to Catalog
item.description: CISA has added one new vulnerability to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) Catalog , based on evidence of active exploitation. CVE-2026-1340 Ivanti Endpoint Manager Mobile (EPMM) Code Injection Vulnerability Th...

item.title: Shrinking the IAM Attack Surface through Identity Visibility and Intelligence Platforms (IVIP)
item.description: The Fragmented State of Modern Enterprise Identity Enterprise IAM is approaching a breaking point. As organizations scale, identity becomes increasingly fragmented across thousands of applications, decentralized teams...

item.title: Anthropic Launches Project Glasswing to Use AI to Find and Fix Critical Software Vulnerabilities
item.description: Anthropic launches Project Glasswing, using its Claude Mythos Preview AI to autonomously identify and fix undiscovered vulnerabilities in critical software

item.title: Python Supply-Chain Compromise
item.description: This is news : A malicious supply chain compromise has been identified in the Python Package Index package litellm version 1.82.8. The published wheel contains a malicious .pth file (litellm_init.pth, 34,628 bytes) wh...

item.title: US Thwarts DNS Hijacking Network Controlled by Russian APT28 Hackers
item.description: The FBI deployed a method to unplug US-based routers compromised by APT28 from the threat actor’s malicious network

item.title: Anthropic's Claude Mythos Finds Thousands of Zero-Day Flaws Across Major Systems
item.description: Artificial Intelligence (AI) company Anthropic announced a new cybersecurity initiative called Project Glasswing that will use a preview version of its new frontier model, Claude Mythos, to find and address security v...

item.title: Claude Discovers Apache ActiveMQ Bug Hidden for 13 Years
item.description: Anthropic’s Claude AI has helped researchers find a vulnerability in Apache ActiveMQ Classic

item.title: Iran‑Backed Threat Actors Hit US CNI Providers via Internet‑Facing OT Assets
item.description: CISA has revealed Iranian attacks causing disruption and financial loss at US critical infrastructure firms

item.title: N. Korean Hackers Spread 1,700 Malicious Packages Across npm, PyPI, Go, Rust
item.description: The North Korea-linked persistent campaign known as Contagious Interview has spread its tentacles by publishing malicious packages targeting the Go, Rust, and PHP ecosystems. "The threat actor's packages were designed...

item.title: TrueConf Zero-Day Attack
item.description: What is the Attack? Operation TrueChaos is a targeted cyber espionage campaign exploiting a zero-day vulnerability in the TrueConf video conferencing platform. The campaign primarily targets government entities in Sou...

item.title: Iran-Linked Hackers Disrupt U.S. Critical Infrastructure by Targeting Internet-Exposed PLCs
item.description: Iran-affiliated cyber actors are targeting internet-facing operational technology (OT) devices across critical infrastructures in the U.S., including programmable logic controllers (PLCs), cybersecurity and intelligen...

item.title: How to use AI to manage cloud security threats
item.description: Cloud defenders constantly face fast-moving security threats.

item.title: Cracks in the Bedrock: Escaping the AWS AgentCore Sandbox
item.description: Unit 42 uncovers critical vulnerabilities in Amazon Bedrock AgentCore's sandbox, demonstrating DNS tunneling and credential exposure. The post Cracks in the Bedrock: Escaping the AWS AgentCore Sandbox appeared first o...

item.title: Storm-1175 Deploys Medusa Ransomware at 'High Velocity'
item.description: Microsoft says the financially motivated cybercrime group has exploited n-day and zero-day vulnerabilities in campaigns predicated on speed.

item.title: Grafana Patches AI Bug That Could Have Leaked User Data
item.description: By hiding malicious instructions on an attacker-controlled Web page, AI could ingest orders that appear benign but return sensitive data to the attacker's server.

item.title: Building AI defenses at scale: Before the threats emerge
item.description: At AWS, we’ve spent decades developing processes and tools that enable us to defend millions of customers simultaneously, wherever they operate around the world. AI has been an extremely helpful addition to the automa...

item.title: Russia Hacked Routers to Steal Microsoft Office Tokens
item.description: Hackers linked to Russia's military intelligence units are using known flaws in older Internet routers to mass harvest authentication tokens from Microsoft Office users, security experts warned today. The spying campa...

item.title: Russian State-Linked APT28 Exploits SOHO Routers in Global DNS Hijacking Campaign
item.description: The Russia-linked threat actor known as APT28 (aka Forest Blizzard) has been linked to a new campaign that has compromised insecure MikroTik and TP-Link routers and modified their settings to turn them into malicious...

item.title: [Webinar] How to Close Identity Gaps in 2026 Before AI Exploits Enterprise Risk
item.description: In the rapid evolution of the 2026 threat landscape, a frustrating paradox has emerged for CISOs and security leaders: Identity programs are maturing, yet the risk is actually increasing. According to new research fro...

item.title: Russian APT28 Hackers Hijack Routers to Steal Credentials, UK Security Agency Warns
item.description: Newly identified malicious campaigns are linked to virtual private servers modified by APT28 to operate as malicious DNS servers
